sampleText:
It seems that the proliferation of artificial intelligence in academic writing has fundamentally transformed how scholars approach their craft. The technology has moved quickly from a distant novelty to an ordinary tool, and that shift has changed how researchers draft, revise, and evaluate their work.

The discussion is no longer only about whether AI should be used. It is about how it should be used, where its limits should be drawn, and how writers can preserve clarity, ownership, and voice while still benefiting from the speed it offers.

The rise of these systems has also altered the rhythm of scholarly composition. Writers can now generate summaries, test outlines, and compare phrasings in seconds, yet those efficiencies do not remove the need for judgment. In many cases, they make judgment more important, because the first clear answer is not always the most precise one.

Researchers across disciplines are grappling with questions of authorship, originality, and intellectual integrity in ways that were perhaps unimaginable just a decade ago. Some scholars see the tools as a practical extension of writing labor, while others worry that the convenience of automation may flatten the specificity of academic voice.

Furthermore, the integration of these tools into the writing process has perhaps blurred the boundaries between human creativity and machine assistance, raising essential questions about the future of scholarly communication. Universities, journals, and individual writers are still deciding how to define responsible use, and those decisions will shape the norms that follow.

document.title: Writing in the age of AI

userBaseline.name: Eleanor Vance
userBaseline.voiceSummary: Direct, declarative, sparing with hedges. Favors em-dashes and short paragraphs.

profile.name: Eleanor Vance
profile.voiceSummary: Direct, declarative, sparing with hedges. Favors em-dashes and short paragraphs.

metrics.name: Sentence Flow
metrics.observation: Your sentences are typically short and direct. This paragraph has several long, complex sentences.

metrics.name: Word Choice
metrics.observation: You rarely use formal academic vocabulary. This passage uses several formal terms like 'proliferation,' 'unprecedented,' and 'unimaginable.'

metrics.name: Structure
metrics.observation: You usually write short paragraphs with clear transitions. This is a single dense paragraph.

metrics.name: Tone
metrics.observation: You typically write with confidence. This passage uses hedging language like 'seems,' 'perhaps,' and 'may have.'

metrics.name: Punctuation
metrics.observation: You often use em-dashes for asides. This paragraph has none.

suggestions.excerpt: It seems that the proliferation of artificial intelligence...
suggestions.targetText: It seems that the proliferation of artificial intelligence in academic writing has fundamentally transformed how scholars approach their craft.
suggestions.observation: You typically write with confidence. The opener hedges with 'seems,' which softens the authority of the claim.
suggestions.proposed: The proliferation of artificial intelligence in academic writing has fundamentally transformed how scholars approach their craft.

suggestions.targetText: Some scholars see the tools as a practical extension of writing labor, while others worry that the convenience of automation may flatten the specificity of academic voice.
suggestions.observation: You typically prefer more natural and direct phrasing. “Writing labor” feels more theoretical than your normal vocabulary.

tradeoff.loss: Reduced rhetorical weight in a list of abstract concepts.

suggestions.proposed: Some scholars see the tools as a practical extension of the writing process, while others worry that the convenience of automation may flatten the specificity of academic voice.

suggestions.excerpt: ...and compare phrasings in seconds, yet those efficiencies do not remove the need for judgment.
suggestions.targetText: Writers can now generate summaries, test outlines, and compare phrasings in seconds, yet those efficiencies do not remove the need for judgment.
suggestions.observation: You typically separate major moves more clearly. Here, the point stays embedded in the paragraph.

tradeoff.gain: Clearer structural pacing and a more deliberate progression.
tradeoff.loss: A slightly less continuous argumentative flow.

suggestions.proposed: Writers can now generate summaries, test outlines, and compare phrasings in seconds. Yet, those efficiencies do not remove the need for judgment.

suggestions.excerpt: Furthermore, the integration of these tools into the writing process has perhaps blurred the boundaries...
suggestions.targetText: Furthermore, the integration of these tools into the writing process has perhaps blurred the boundaries between human creativity and machine assistance, raising essential questions about the future of scholarly communication.
suggestions.observation: You usually use em-dashes for asides. Here, the aside is tucked into commas instead.

tradeoff.gain: Visual cue that matches your signature punctuation.
tradeoff.loss: A more neutral, less personal cadence.

suggestions.proposed: The integration of these tools — quietly, over years — has blurred the boundaries between human creativity and machine assistance.
suggestions.proposedPreview: The integration of these tools — quietly, over years — has blurred the boundaries...

suggestions.excerpt: The rise of these systems has also altered the rhythm of scholarly composition...
suggestions.targetText: The rise of these systems has also altered the rhythm of scholarly composition.
suggestions.observation: You usually sound more direct. This line keeps a more detached, analytical distance.

tradeoff.loss: Some of the measured distance in the original framing.

suggestions.proposed: The rise of these systems has changed scholarly composition in practical ways.

suggestions.excerpt: ...the convenience of automation may flatten the specificity of academic voice.
suggestions.targetText: Some scholars see the tools as a practical extension of writing labor, while others worry that the convenience of automation may flatten the specificity of academic voice.
suggestions.observation: You usually choose plainer verbs and nouns. This phrasing leans more abstract than your baseline.
suggestions.proposed: Some scholars see the tools as a practical extension of writing labor, while others worry that automation may dull the distinctiveness of academic voice.
suggestions.proposedPreview: ...the convenience of automation may dull the distinctiveness of academic voice.

suggestions.excerpt: In many cases, they make judgment more important, because the first clear answer is not always the most precise one.
suggestions.targetText: In many cases, they make judgment more important, because the first clear answer is not always the most precise one.
suggestions.observation: You typically end paragraphs more cleanly. This sentence would land better with more separation around it.
suggestions.proposed: In many cases, they make judgment more important. The first clear answer is not always the most precise one.

suggestions.excerpt: Some scholars see the tools as a practical extension of writing labor, while others worry that the convenience of automation may flatten the specificity of academic voice.
suggestions.targetText: Some scholars see the tools as a practical extension of writing labor, while others worry that the convenience of automation may flatten the specificity of academic voice.
suggestions.observation: You usually write with more certainty. This sentence leaves more hedging and distance in place.

tradeoff.loss: Less room for uncertainty in the comparison.

suggestions.proposed: Some scholars see the tools as a practical extension of writing labor, while others worry that automation will flatten academic voice.

suggestions.excerpt: Writers can now generate summaries, test outlines, and compare phrasings in seconds, yet those efficiencies do not remove the need for judgment.

suggestions.observation: You typically break dense ideas earlier. This sentence keeps both clauses together, which makes it feel heavier.
suggestions.proposed: Writers can now generate summaries, test outlines, and compare phrasings in seconds. Those efficiencies do not remove the need for judgment.

suggestions.excerpt: Universities, journals, and individual writers are still deciding how to define responsible use...
suggestions.targetText: Universities, journals, and individual writers are still deciding how to define responsible use, and those decisions will shape the norms that follow.
suggestions.observation: You usually prefer more direct phrasing. This version is accurate, but more formal and generalized than your norm.
suggestions.proposed: Universities, journals, and individual writers are still deciding what responsible use should mean.
suggestions.proposedPreview: Universities, journals, and individual writers are still deciding what responsible use should mean...

suggestions.excerpt: It is about how it should be used, where its limits should be drawn, and how writers can preserve clarity, ownership, and voice...
suggestions.targetText: It is about how it should be used, where its limits should be drawn, and how writers can preserve clarity, ownership, and voice while still benefiting from the speed it offers.
suggestions.observation: You usually mark pauses with em-dashes. This sentence relies on commas instead of your usual punctuation pattern.

tradeoff.gain: A more distinctive pause and a stronger cadence.
tradeoff.loss: A slightly more marked stylistic signature.

suggestions.proposed: It is about how it should be used — where its limits should be drawn — and how writers can preserve clarity, ownership, and voice while still benefiting from the speed it offers.
suggestions.proposedPreview: It is about how it should be used — where its limits should be drawn — and how writers can preserve clarity, ownership, and voice...

suggestions.excerpt: The rise of these systems has also altered the rhythm of scholarly composition.
suggestions.targetText: The rise of these systems has also altered the rhythm of scholarly composition.
suggestions.observation: You typically use shorter structural units. This idea is sitting inside the paragraph rather than guiding the transition.

tradeoff.loss: Slightly less continuity within the paragraph.

suggestions.proposed: The rise of these systems has also altered the rhythm of scholarly composition. That change is visible in how writers begin and revise drafts.

suggestions.excerpt: The discussion is no longer only about whether AI should be used.
suggestions.targetText: The discussion is no longer only about whether AI should be used.
suggestions.observation: You usually sound more decisive. This line is clear, but it stays slightly more tentative than your baseline.
suggestions.proposed: For that reason, the discussion is no longer only about whether AI should be used. It is about how it should be used.

suggestions.excerpt: The discussion is no longer only about whether AI should be used. It is about how it should be used...
suggestions.targetText: The discussion is no longer only about whether AI should be used. It is about how it should be used, where its limits should be drawn, and how writers can preserve clarity, ownership, and voice while still benefiting from the speed it offers.
suggestions.observation: You usually keep closings more compact. This repetition is deliberate, but it reads a little slower than your norm.
suggestions.proposed: The discussion is no longer about whether AI should be used. It is about how it should be used.

suggestions.excerpt: Writers can now generate summaries, test outlines, and compare phrasings in seconds.
suggestions.targetText: Writers can now generate summaries, test outlines, and compare phrasings in seconds, yet those efficiencies do not remove the need for judgment.
suggestions.observation: You typically let important claims stand apart more clearly. Here, the sentence stays embedded in the flow.
suggestions.proposed: Writers can now generate summaries, test outlines, and compare phrasings in seconds. That speed changes the writing process itself.

suggestions.excerpt: The rise of these systems has also altered the rhythm of scholarly composition.
suggestions.targetText: The rise of these systems has also altered the rhythm of scholarly composition.
suggestions.observation: You usually lean on stronger punctuation breaks. This line uses a softer pause than your normal style.

tradeoff.gain: A more precise and balanced pause.

suggestions.proposed: The rise of these systems has also altered the rhythm of scholarly composition; that change matters.
